revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''磯長谷古墳群'''（いそながたにこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]にある[[天皇陵]]を中心…'

revision.text:
{{加筆|'''その他の古墳'''項目が、<nowiki>{{節stub}}</nowiki>のままです。情報が足りません。'''概要'''項目も、情報があれば加筆願います。|date=2009年3月11日}} '''磯長谷古墳群'''（いそながたにこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]にある、[[天皇陵]]を中心とした[[古墳]]群である。
==概要== 磯長谷古墳群は[[宮内庁]]から治定されている[[天皇陵]]4基（[[太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵]]・[[春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵]]・[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]・[[山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵]]）と、[[叡福寺#叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子陵墓]]など約30基からなる古墳群である。これらは、6世紀中ごろから7世紀前半の[[古墳時代]]末期に造られたと見られる。
この古墳群が上空から[[円形]]に見える事などが理由で、俗に「'''梅鉢御陵'''」（うめはちごりょう）と呼ばれる。また、この地一帯は皇族の陵墓が集中していることから、「王陵の谷」とも呼ばれる。
===主な古墳=== {| class="wikitable" ! 名 称 !! 埋葬者 !! 墳形 !! 墳丘長 !! 国史跡指定 |- | [[太子西山古墳]] || [[敏達天皇]] || 前方後円墳 || 93m || [[天皇陵]] |- | [[春日向山古墳]] || [[用明天皇]] || 方墳 || 63m×60m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田高塚古墳]] || [[推古天皇]] ||方墳 || 63m×56m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田上ノ山古墳]]|| [[孝徳天皇]] || 円墳 || 30m || 天皇陵 |- | [[叡福寺#叡福寺北古墳|叡福寺北古墳]] || [[聖徳太子]] || 円墳 || 55m || 天皇陵 |}
===その他の古墳=== {{節stub}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#埼玉県|日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html 磯長谷古墳群]（近つ飛鳥博物館ウェブサイト）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いそなかたにこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の史跡]] [[Category:太子町 (大阪府)]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

'''磯長谷古墳群'''（いそながたにこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]にある、[[天皇陵]]を中心とした[[古墳]]群である。
==概要== 磯長谷古墳群は[[宮内庁]]から治定されている[[天皇陵]]4基（[[太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵]]・[[春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵]]・[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]・[[山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵]]）と、[[叡福寺#叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子御廟]]など約30基からなる古墳群である。これらは、前方後円墳の造営が禁止された後の6世紀中ごろから7世紀前半にかけての[[古墳時代]]末期から[[飛鳥時代]]初期のものと見られる。（ただし、敏達天皇陵は磯長谷では唯一の前方後円墳である。）
この古墳群が上空から[[円形]]に見える事などが理由で、俗に「'''梅鉢御陵'''」（うめはちごりょう）と呼ばれる。なお、この地一帯は[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の斜面が幅広い谷地形を作っており、磯長谷と呼ばれている。さらに皇族の陵墓が集中していることから、「王陵の谷」とも呼ばれる。
===主な古墳=== 下記の5つの古墳が宮内庁から[[天皇陵|陵墓]]に指定されている。 {| class="wikitable" ! 名 称 !! 埋葬者 !! 墳形 !! 墳丘長 !! 備考 |- | [[太子西山古墳]] || [[敏達天皇]] || 前方後円墳 || 93m || 天皇陵 |- | [[春日向山古墳]] || [[用明天皇]] || 方墳 || 63m×60m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田高塚古墳]] || [[推古天皇]] ||方墳 || 63m×56m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田上ノ山古墳]]|| [[孝徳天皇]] || 円墳 || 30m || 天皇陵 |- | [[叡福寺#叡福寺北古墳|叡福寺北古墳]] || [[聖徳太子]] || 円墳 || 55m || 天皇陵クラス |}
===その他の古墳=== ;おもな古墳 *二子塚古墳 :推古天皇陵（春日向山古墳）の南東約200mに位置する[[双方墳]]（長方墳とも言われる）。長辺約60m、短辺約25m。墳丘高さ約6〜4.8mの墳丘それぞれにほぼ同形同規模の横穴式石室があるとみられる。推古天皇と[[竹田皇子]]の合葬陵との伝承がある。 *越前塚古墳 :東西約75m、南北約55m、墳丘高さ約8mの長方墳。西側は墳丘が大きく破壊されている。越前塚に寄り添うように、 :*モンド神塚古墳（円墳） :*かまど塚古墳（円墳） :*石塚古墳（不明） :*陪塚（大古墳に附属する小墳丘） :の小古墳がある。
==備考== 磯長谷には「奥城古墳」とよばれる名称の古墳があるが、太子西山古墳（敏達天皇陵）の別名<small>（参考：[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html 太子町ウェブサイト]）</small>とも聖徳太子御廟の名称としている文献もある。
== 参考文献 == *河内飛鳥を訪ねてみよう（石部政志 編著、松籟社 ISBN 4-87984-101-3）
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#埼玉県|日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html 磯長谷古墳群]（近つ飛鳥博物館ウェブサイト）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いそなかたにこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の史跡]] [[Category:太子町 (大阪府)]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

revision.comment: 今のところ前方後円墳の造営が禁止されたという事実は確認できない

==概要== 磯長谷古墳群は[[宮内庁]]から治定されている[[天皇陵]]4基（[[太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵]]・[[春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵]]・[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]・[[山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵]]）と、[[叡福寺#叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子廟]]など約30基からなる古墳群である。これらは、前方後円墳の造営が終了した後の6世紀中ごろから7世紀前半にかけての[[古墳時代]]末期から[[飛鳥時代]]初期のものと見られる。（ただし、敏達天皇陵は磯長谷では唯一の前方後円墳である。）
天皇陵4基と聖徳太子廟の5つの古墳は、梅の花びらになぞらえて「'''梅鉢御陵'''」（うめばちごりょう）と総称される。なお、この地一帯は[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の斜面が幅広い谷地形を作っており、磯長谷と呼ばれている。さらに皇族の陵墓が集中していることから、「'''王陵の谷'''」とも呼ばれる。

===その他の古墳=== ;おもな古墳 *二子塚古墳 :推古天皇陵（春日向山古墳）の南東約200mに位置する[[双方墳]]（長方墳とも言われる）。長辺約60m、短辺約25m。墳丘高さ約6〜4.8mの墳丘それぞれにほぼ同形同規模の横穴式石室があるとみられる。推古天皇と[[竹田皇子]]の合葬陵との伝承がある。 *越前塚古墳 :東西約75m、南北約55m、墳丘高さ約8mの長方墳。西側は墳丘が大きく破壊されている。越前塚に寄り添うように、 :*モンド神塚古墳（円墳） :*かまど塚古墳（円墳） :*石塚古墳（不明） :*陪塚（大古墳に附属する小墳丘） :の小古墳がある。

'''磯長谷古墳群'''（しながだにこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]にある、[[天皇陵]]を中心とした[[古墳]]群である。
==概要== 磯長谷古墳群は[[宮内庁]]から治定されている[[天皇陵]]4基（[[太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵]]・[[春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵]]・[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]・[[山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵]]）と、[[叡福寺#叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子廟]]など約30基からなる古墳群である。これらは、前方後円墳の造営が終了した後の6世紀中ごろから7世紀前半にかけての[[古墳時代]]末期から[[飛鳥時代]]初期のものと見られる。（ただし、敏達天皇陵は磯長谷では唯一の前方後円墳である。）
天皇陵4基と聖徳太子廟の5つの古墳は、梅の花びらになぞらえて「'''梅鉢御陵'''」（うめばちごりょう）と総称される。なお、この地一帯は[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の斜面が幅広い谷地形を作っており、磯長谷と呼ばれている。さらに皇族の陵墓が集中していることから、「'''王陵の谷'''」とも呼ばれる。
===主な古墳=== 下記の5つの古墳が宮内庁から[[天皇陵|陵墓]]に指定されている。 {| class="wikitable" ! 名 称 !! 埋葬者 !! 墳形 !! 墳丘長 !! 備考 |- | [[太子西山古墳]] || [[敏達天皇]] || 前方後円墳 || 93m || 天皇陵 |- | [[春日向山古墳]] || [[用明天皇]] || 方墳 || 63m×60m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田高塚古墳]] || [[推古天皇]] ||方墳 || 63m×56m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田上ノ山古墳]]|| [[孝徳天皇]] || 円墳 || 30m || 天皇陵 |- | [[叡福寺#叡福寺北古墳|叡福寺北古墳]] || [[聖徳太子]] || 円墳 || 55m || 天皇陵クラス |}
===その他の古墳=== ;おもな古墳 *二子塚古墳 :推古天皇陵（春日向山古墳）の南東約200mに位置する[[双方墳]]（長方墳とも言われる）。長辺約60m、短辺約25m。墳丘高さ約6〜4.8mの墳丘それぞれにほぼ同形同規模の横穴式石室があるとみられる。推古天皇と[[竹田皇子]]の合葬陵との伝承がある。 *越前塚古墳 :東西約75m、南北約55m、墳丘高さ約8mの長方墳。西側は墳丘が大きく破壊されている。越前塚に寄り添うように、 :*モンド神塚古墳（円墳） :*かまど塚古墳（円墳） :*石塚古墳（不明） :*陪塚（大古墳に附属する小墳丘） :の小古墳がある。
==備考== 磯長谷には「奥城古墳」とよばれる名称の古墳があるが、太子西山古墳（敏達天皇陵）の別名<small>（参考：[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html 太子町ウェブサイト]）</small>とも聖徳太子廟の名称としている文献もある。
== 参考文献 == *河内飛鳥を訪ねてみよう（石部政志 編著、松籟社 ISBN 4-87984-101-3）
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#埼玉県|日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html 磯長谷古墳群]（近つ飛鳥博物館ウェブサイト）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかたにこふんくん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府の歴史]] [[Category:太子町 (大阪府)]]

== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#埼玉県|日本の古墳一覧]]

revision.text:
'''磯長谷古墳群'''（しながだにこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]にある、[[天皇陵]]を中心とした[[古墳]]群である。
==概要== 磯長谷古墳群は[[宮内庁]]から治定されている[[天皇陵]]4基（[[太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵]]・[[春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵]]・[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]・[[山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵]]）と、[[叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓]]など約30基からなる古墳群である。これらは、前方後円墳の造営が終了した後の6世紀中ごろから7世紀前半にかけての[[古墳時代]]末期から[[飛鳥時代]]初期のものと見られる。（ただし、敏達天皇陵は磯長谷では唯一の前方後円墳である。）
天皇陵4基と聖徳太子廟の5つの古墳は、梅の花びらになぞらえて「'''梅鉢御陵'''」（うめばちごりょう）と総称される。なお、この地一帯は[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の斜面が幅広い谷地形を作っており、磯長谷と呼ばれている。さらに皇族の陵墓が集中していることから、「'''王陵の谷'''」とも呼ばれる。
===主な古墳=== 下記の5つの古墳が宮内庁から[[天皇陵|陵墓]]に指定されている。 {| class="wikitable" ! 名 称 !! 埋葬者 !! 墳形 !! 墳丘長 !! 備考 |- | [[太子西山古墳]] || [[敏達天皇]] || 前方後円墳 || 93m || 天皇陵 |- | [[春日向山古墳]] || [[用明天皇]] || 方墳 || 63m×60m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田高塚古墳]] || [[推古天皇]] ||方墳 || 63m×56m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田上ノ山古墳]]|| [[孝徳天皇]] || 円墳 || 30m || 天皇陵 |- | [[叡福寺北古墳]] || [[聖徳太子]] || 円墳 || 55m || 天皇陵クラス |}
===その他の古墳=== ;おもな古墳 *[[二子塚古墳 (太子町)|二子塚古墳]] :推古天皇陵（春日向山古墳）の南東約200mに位置する[[双方墳]]（長方墳とも言われる）。長辺約60m、短辺約25m。墳丘高さ約6〜4.8mの墳丘それぞれにほぼ同形同規模の横穴式石室があるとみられる。推古天皇と[[竹田皇子]]の合葬陵との伝承がある。 *[[葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳]] :東西約75m、南北約55m、墳丘高さ約8mの長方墳。西側は墳丘が大きく破壊されている。越前塚に寄り添うように、 :*モンド神塚古墳（円墳） :*[[釜戸塚古墳|かまど塚古墳]]（円墳） :*[[葉室石塚古墳|石塚古墳]]（不明） :*陪塚（大古墳に附属する小墳丘） :の小古墳がある。
==備考== 磯長谷には「奥城古墳」とよばれる名称の古墳があるが、太子西山古墳（敏達天皇陵）の別名<small>（参考：[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html 太子町ウェブサイト]）</small>とも聖徳太子廟の名称としている文献もある。
== 参考文献 == *河内飛鳥を訪ねてみよう（石部政志 編著、松籟社 ISBN 4-87984-101-3）
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#埼玉県|日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html 磯長谷古墳群]（近つ飛鳥博物館ウェブサイト）

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

[[category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[category:Isonokami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:磯長谷古墳群]]

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)

The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."

Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}

== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "ew pages]]" to "ull category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar"
revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."

It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. '''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
