'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."

Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}

It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)

== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus|ja|春日向山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Tumulus|ja|山田高塚古墳}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Tumulus|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Tumulus|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Tumulus (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Tumulus (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Tumulus|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Tumulus|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Tumulus|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Tumulus|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Tumulus," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Tumulus|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."

It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. '''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."

Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.

The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}} == See Also! == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Isonokami Valley Tumuli] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum website)

'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.

These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓|12=draft}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳|12=draft}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of Kofuns centered around {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as imperial tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only keyhole-shaped mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) within this group.
The five main Kofuns, modeled after the petals of a plum blossom, are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). This area, located along the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is called "Shinagadani" due to its wide valley geography. Owing to the concentration of imperial tombs, the area is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns ===
The following five Kofuns are designated as imperial mausoleums by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred_individual !! Type !! Size !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Bidatsu || Keyhole-shaped mound || 93m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || Emperor Yōmei || Square mound || 63m × 60m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft}} || Empress Suiko || Square mound || 63m × 56m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Kōtoku || Round mound || 30m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || Prince Shōtoku || Round mound || 55m || Comparable to Imperial Mausoleums |}
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a rectangular mound) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)
