== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳|12=draft}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Masashi Ishibe|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|publisher=Shōraisha|isbn=4-87984-101-3}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Isonokami Valley Kofun Group]] to [[Shinga Valley Kofuns]]: Text replacement - "Isonokami Valley Kofun Group" to "Shinga Valley Kofuns"
revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of Kofuns centered around {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as imperial tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only keyhole-shaped mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) within this group.
The five main Kofuns, modeled after the petals of a plum blossom, are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). This area, located along the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is called "Shinagadani" due to its wide valley geography. Owing to the concentration of imperial tombs, the area is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns ===
The following five Kofuns are designated as imperial mausoleums by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred_individual !! Type !! Size !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Bidatsu || Keyhole-shaped mound || 93m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || Emperor Yōmei || Square mound || 63m × 60m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft}} || Empress Suiko || Square mound || 63m × 56m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Kōtoku || Round mound || 30m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || Prince Shōtoku || Round mound || 55m || Comparable to Imperial Mausoleums |}
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a rectangular mound) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}

'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of Kofuns centered around {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.

These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only keyhole-shaped mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) within this group.
The five main Kofuns, modeled after the petals of a plum blossom, are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). This area, located along the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is called "Shinagadani" due to its wide valley geography. Owing to the concentration of imperial tombs, the area is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns ===
The following five Kofuns are designated as imperial mausoleums by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred_individual !! Type !! Size !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Bidatsu || Keyhole-shaped mound || 93m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || Emperor Yōmei || Square mound || 63m × 60m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft}} || Empress Suiko || Square mound || 63m × 56m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Kōtoku || Round mound || 30m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || Prince Shōtoku || Round mound || 55m || Comparable to Imperial Mausoleums |}
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a rectangular mound) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳|12=draft}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Shinga Valley Kofuns]] to [[Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]]: Text replacement - "Shinga Valley Kofuns" to "Shinagadani Kofun Cluster"
revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of Kofuns centered around {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as imperial tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only keyhole-shaped mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) within this group.
The five main Kofuns, modeled after the petals of a plum blossom, are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). This area, located along the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is called "Shinagadani" due to its wide valley geography. Owing to the concentration of imperial tombs, the area is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns ===
The following five Kofuns are designated as imperial mausoleums by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred_individual !! Type !! Size !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Bidatsu || Keyhole-shaped mound || 93m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || Emperor Yōmei || Square mound || 63m × 60m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft}} || Empress Suiko || Square mound || 63m × 56m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Kōtoku || Round mound || 30m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || Prince Shōtoku || Round mound || 55m || Comparable to Imperial Mausoleums |}
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a rectangular mound) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."

Length !! revision.comment: Text replacement - "|1=Kasugayama Kofun|" to "|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|"
revision.text:
'''Isonokami Valley Tumuli''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of {{ill|tumuli|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|lt=Imperial mausolea|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of Kofuns centered around {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Japan|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as imperial tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only keyhole-shaped mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) within this group.
The five main Kofuns, modeled after the petals of a plum blossom, are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). This area, located along the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is called "Shinagadani" due to its wide valley geography. Owing to the concentration of imperial tombs, the area is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns ===
The following five Kofuns are designated as imperial mausoleums by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred_individual !! Type !! Size !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Bidatsu || Keyhole-shaped mound || 93m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || Emperor Yōmei || Square mound || 63m × 60m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft}} || Empress Suiko || Square mound || 63m × 56m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || Emperor Kōtoku || Round mound || 30m || Imperial Mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || Prince Shōtoku || Round mound || 55m || Comparable to Imperial Mausoleums |}
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a rectangular mound) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

== Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}} || Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} || Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}} || Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}} || {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} || Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |}
=== Other Tumuli === Other notable tumuli include: ; {{ill|Futagotsuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukohoyama Kofun (Empress Suiko's mausoleum), this is a {{ill|paired rectangular tumulus|ja|双方墳|12=draft}} (sometimes referred to as an elongated square tumulus). It measures about 60m by 25m, with two similarly sized horizontal stone chambers within. It is traditionally believed to be the joint burial site of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular tumulus measuring approximately 75m east-west by 55m north-south, with a height of about 8m. The western side is heavily damaged. Surrounding tumuli include: :* {{ill|Mondo Shrine Kofun|ja|モンド神塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (round tumulus) :* {{ill|Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (small tumuli related to larger ones)
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|ja|天皇陵|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. == Overview == The Isonokami Valley Tumuli include four Imperial mausolea designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}) along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|12=draft}} and approximately 30 other tumuli. These tumuli date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, corresponding to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft}} in this area.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Goryō'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''."
== Overview ==
The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as imperial tombs by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|12=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the Taishi Nishiyama Kofun is the only keyhole-shaped mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) within this group.
The five main Kofuns, modeled after the petals of a plum blossom, are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). This area, located along the foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is called "Shinagadani" due to its wide valley geography. Owing to the concentration of imperial tombs, the area is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).

Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>

'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|ja|天皇陵|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|12=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|12=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}} is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}} is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft|12=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|12=draft|12=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|12=draft}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|12=draft|12=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

revision.comment: 15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:磯長谷古墳群 up to rev 70664932
revision.text:
'''磯長谷古墳群'''（しながだにこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]にある、[[天皇陵]]を中心とした[[古墳]]群である。
==概要== 磯長谷古墳群は[[宮内庁]]から治定されている[[天皇陵]]4基（[[太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵]]・[[春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵]]・[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]・[[山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵]]）と、[[叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓]]など約30基からなる古墳群である。これらは、前方後円墳の造営が終了した後の6世紀中ごろから7世紀前半にかけての[[古墳時代]]末期から[[飛鳥時代]]初期のものと見られる。（ただし、敏達天皇陵は磯長谷では唯一の前方後円墳である。）
天皇陵4基と聖徳太子廟の5つの古墳は、梅の花びらになぞらえて「'''梅鉢御陵'''」（うめばちごりょう）と総称される。なお、この地一帯は[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の斜面が幅広い谷地形を作っており、磯長谷と呼ばれている。さらに皇族の陵墓が集中していることから、「'''王陵の谷'''」とも呼ばれる。
===主な古墳=== 下記の5つの古墳が宮内庁から[[天皇陵|陵墓]]に指定されている。 {| class="wikitable" ! 名 称 !! 埋葬者 !! 墳形 !! 墳丘長 !! 備考 |- | [[太子西山古墳]] || [[敏達天皇]] || 前方後円墳 || 93m || 天皇陵 |- | [[春日向山古墳]] || [[用明天皇]] || 方墳 || 63m×60m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田高塚古墳]] || [[推古天皇]] ||方墳 || 63m×56m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田上ノ山古墳]]|| [[孝徳天皇]] || 円墳 || 30m || 天皇陵 |- | [[叡福寺北古墳]] || [[聖徳太子]] || 円墳 || 55m || 天皇陵クラス |}
===その他の古墳=== おもな古墳
; [[二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|二子塚古墳]] : 推古天皇陵（春日向山古墳）の南東約200mに位置する[[双方墳]]（長方墳とも言われる）。長辺約60m、短辺約25m。墳丘高さ約6〜4.8mの墳丘それぞれにほぼ同形同規模の横穴式石室があるとみられる。推古天皇と[[竹田皇子]]の合葬陵との伝承がある。 ; [[葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳]] : 東西約75m、南北約55m、墳丘高さ約8mの長方墳。西側は墳丘が大きく破壊されている。越前塚に寄り添うように、 :* モンド神塚古墳（円墳） :* [[釜戸塚古墳|かまど塚古墳]]（円墳） :* [[葉室石塚古墳|石塚古墳]]（不明） :* 陪塚（大古墳に附属する小墳丘） : の小古墳がある。
==備考== 磯長谷には「奥城古墳」とよばれる名称の古墳があるが、太子西山古墳（敏達天皇陵）の別名<small>（参考：[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html 太子町ウェブサイト]）</small>とも聖徳太子廟の名称としている文献もある。
== 参考文献 == *河内飛鳥を訪ねてみよう（石部政志 編著、松籟社 ISBN 4-87984-101-3）
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#埼玉県|日本の古墳一覧]]

{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかたにこふんくん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群|*]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[磯長谷古墳群]] to [[Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''磯長谷古墳群'''（しながだにこふんぐん）は[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]にある、[[天皇陵]]を中心とした[[古墳]]群である。
==概要== 磯長谷古墳群は[[宮内庁]]から治定されている[[天皇陵]]4基（[[太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵]]・[[春日向山古墳|用明天皇陵]]・[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]・[[山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵]]）と、[[叡福寺北古墳|聖徳太子墓]]など約30基からなる古墳群である。これらは、前方後円墳の造営が終了した後の6世紀中ごろから7世紀前半にかけての[[古墳時代]]末期から[[飛鳥時代]]初期のものと見られる。（ただし、敏達天皇陵は磯長谷では唯一の前方後円墳である。）
天皇陵4基と聖徳太子廟の5つの古墳は、梅の花びらになぞらえて「'''梅鉢御陵'''」（うめばちごりょう）と総称される。なお、この地一帯は[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]山麓の斜面が幅広い谷地形を作っており、磯長谷と呼ばれている。さらに皇族の陵墓が集中していることから、「'''王陵の谷'''」とも呼ばれる。
===主な古墳=== 下記の5つの古墳が宮内庁から[[天皇陵|陵墓]]に指定されている。 {| class="wikitable" ! 名 称 !! 埋葬者 !! 墳形 !! 墳丘長 !! 備考 |- | [[太子西山古墳]] || [[敏達天皇]] || 前方後円墳 || 93m || 天皇陵 |- | [[春日向山古墳]] || [[用明天皇]] || 方墳 || 63m×60m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田高塚古墳]] || [[推古天皇]] ||方墳 || 63m×56m || 天皇陵 |- | [[山田上ノ山古墳]]|| [[孝徳天皇]] || 円墳 || 30m || 天皇陵 |- | [[叡福寺北古墳]] || [[聖徳太子]] || 円墳 || 55m || 天皇陵クラス |}
===その他の古墳=== おもな古墳
; [[二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|二子塚古墳]] : 推古天皇陵（春日向山古墳）の南東約200mに位置する[[双方墳]]（長方墳とも言われる）。長辺約60m、短辺約25m。墳丘高さ約6〜4.8mの墳丘それぞれにほぼ同形同規模の横穴式石室があるとみられる。推古天皇と[[竹田皇子]]の合葬陵との伝承がある。 ; [[葉室塚古墳|越前塚古墳]] : 東西約75m、南北約55m、墳丘高さ約8mの長方墳。西側は墳丘が大きく破壊されている。越前塚に寄り添うように、 :* モンド神塚古墳（円墳） :* [[釜戸塚古墳|かまど塚古墳]]（円墳） :* [[葉室石塚古墳|石塚古墳]]（不明） :* 陪塚（大古墳に附属する小墳丘） : の小古墳がある。
==備考== 磯長谷には「奥城古墳」とよばれる名称の古墳があるが、太子西山古墳（敏達天皇陵）の別名<small>（参考：[http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html 太子町ウェブサイト]）</small>とも聖徳太子廟の名称としている文献もある。
== 参考文献 == *河内飛鳥を訪ねてみよう（石部政志 編著、松籟社 ISBN 4-87984-101-3）
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#埼玉県|日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html 磯長谷古墳群]（近つ飛鳥博物館ウェブサイト）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかたにこふんくん}} [[Category:磯長谷古墳群|*]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

(王陵の谷).

; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. (王陵の谷).

Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)

[[sn:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:磯長谷古墳群]] [[sn:Isonokami Valley Kofun Group]] [[sn:Isonokami Kofuns]] [[sn:sonokami Kofuns]] [[en:Isonokami Kofuns]]

'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
'''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial tombs|qq=}} located in {{ill|Taishi, Osaka|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}. These Kofuns date to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} to the early {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka period|qq=}}, between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yōmei|qq=}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōtoku|qq=}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|qq=}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|qq=}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://chikatsu.mediajoy.com/jousetsu/shinagadani.html Shinagadani Kofun Group] (Chikatsu Asuka Museum Website)

Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.

The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|ja|敏達天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Bidatsu|qq=}}|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yōmei|qq=}}|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}}|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōtoku|qq=}}|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}}|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :

Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|ja|二子塚古墳 (大阪府太子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|qq=}} : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=}}.
; {{ill|Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|qq=}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|qq=}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)

The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
