[[sn:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]]
[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]] [[sn:Isonokami Valley Kofun Group]] [[sn:Isonokami Kofuns]] [[sn:sonokami Kofuns]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns centered on imperial tombs, located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group consists of four imperial tombs designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]—{{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Bidatsu), {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei), {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko), and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kōtoku)—as well as approximately 30 other Kofuns, including the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}, said to be the tomb of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. These Kofuns date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, spanning the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Notably, the mound attributed to Emperor Bidatsu is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') within this group.
The four imperial tombs and Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum are collectively referred to as the "'''Plum Blossom Petal Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). The area, situated on the broad valley slopes of the {{ill|Nijō Mountains|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Mountains|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is known as Shinagadani. Due to the concentration of royal tombs, it is also called the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns === The following five Kofuns have been designated as [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred Individual !! Mound Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] || Keyhole-shaped || 93 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] || Square || 63 m × 60 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] || Square || 63 m × 56 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] || Round || 30 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] || Round || 55 m || Equivalent to an imperial tomb |}
=== Other Notable Kofuns === ; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Taishi, Osaka)]] : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of Empress Suiko’s tomb (Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun), this mound is a {{ill|paired Kofun|ja|双方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paired Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (or rectangular mound), measuring about 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width. Each mound is believed to contain similar-sized horizontal stone chambers. According to legend, this tomb is shared by Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Echizen Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} : A rectangular mound measuring approximately 75 meters east-to-west, 55 meters north-to-south, and 8 meters in height. The western side of the mound is significantly damaged. Smaller associated Kofuns include: :* Mondo Kamizuka Kofun (round) :* {{ill|Kamado Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} (round) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (undetermined) :* Subordinate mounds associated with larger burials
== Notes == The term "Okutsuki Kofun" is used to describe a Kofun in Shinagadani, which has been alternatively identified as another name for the Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun (Emperor Bidatsu’s tomb)<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website])</small> or Prince Shōtoku’s mausoleum in certain sources.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Taishi|qid=Q1347173}}|part_of={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qid=Q48743902}}; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|qid=Q11371443}}; {{ill|Goryōyama Kofun|qid=Q48753099}}; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Cluster|qid=Q48753076}}; {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qid=Q11513590}}; {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|qid=Q63386720}}; {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qid=Q48751982}}; {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qid=Q11470748}}; {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qid=Q48752311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588120]] Wikidata name: Shinagadani Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}. These Kofuns date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)]] : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==

[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns centered on imperial tombs, located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group consists of four imperial tombs designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]—{{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Bidatsu), {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei), {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko), and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kōtoku)—as well as approximately 30 other Kofuns, including the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}, said to be the tomb of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. These Kofuns date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, spanning the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Notably, the mound attributed to Emperor Bidatsu is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') within this group.
The four imperial tombs and Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum are collectively referred to as the "'''Plum Blossom Petal Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). The area, situated on the broad valley slopes of the {{ill|Nijō Mountains|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Mountains|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is known as Shinagadani. Due to the concentration of royal tombs, it is also called the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns === The following five Kofuns have been designated as [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred Individual !! Mound Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] || Keyhole-shaped || 93 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] || Square || 63 m × 60 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] || Square || 63 m × 56 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] || Round || 30 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] || Round || 55 m || Equivalent to an imperial tomb |}
=== Other Notable Kofuns === ; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Taishi, Osaka)]] : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of Empress Suiko’s tomb (Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun), this mound is a {{ill|paired Kofun|ja|双方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paired Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (or rectangular mound), measuring about 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width. Each mound is believed to contain similar-sized horizontal stone chambers. According to legend, this tomb is shared by Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Echizen Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} : A rectangular mound measuring approximately 75 meters east-to-west, 55 meters north-to-south, and 8 meters in height. The western side of the mound is significantly damaged. Smaller associated Kofuns include: :* Mondo Kamizuka Kofun (round) :* {{ill|Kamado Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} (round) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (undetermined) :* Subordinate mounds associated with larger burials
== Notes == The term "Okutsuki Kofun" is used to describe a Kofun in Shinagadani, which has been alternatively identified as another name for the Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun (Emperor Bidatsu’s tomb)<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website])</small> or Prince Shōtoku’s mausoleum in certain sources.
== References == * {{Cite book|language=ja|title=Visiting Kawachi Asuka|editor=Masashi Ishibe|publisher={{ill|Shōrai-sha|ja|松籟社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōrai-sha|qq=|1=Shoraisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532207}}|isbn=4-87984-101-3}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

[[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11588120}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Taishi|qid=Q1347173}}|part_of={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qid=Q48743902}}; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|qid=Q11371443}}; {{ill|Goryōyama Kofun|qid=Q48753099}}; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Cluster|qid=Q48753076}}; {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qid=Q11513590}}; {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|qid=Q63386720}}; {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qid=Q48751982}}; {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qid=Q11470748}}; {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qid=Q48752311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588120]] Wikidata name: Shinagadani Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}. These Kofuns date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)]] : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)

The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>

[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns centered on imperial tombs, located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group consists of four imperial tombs designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]—{{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Bidatsu), {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei), {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko), and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kōtoku)—as well as approximately 30 other Kofuns, including the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}, said to be the tomb of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. These Kofuns date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, spanning the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Notably, the mound attributed to Emperor Bidatsu is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') within this group.
The four imperial tombs and Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum are collectively referred to as the "'''Plum Blossom Petal Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). The area, situated on the broad valley slopes of the {{ill|Nijō Mountains|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Mountains|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is known as Shinagadani. Due to the concentration of royal tombs, it is also called the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns === The following five Kofuns have been designated as [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred Individual !! Mound Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] || Keyhole-shaped || 93 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] || Square || 63 m × 60 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] || Square || 63 m × 56 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] || Round || 30 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] || Round || 55 m || Equivalent to an imperial tomb |}
=== Other Notable Kofuns === ; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Taishi, Osaka)]] : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of Empress Suiko’s tomb (Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun), this mound is a {{ill|paired Kofun|ja|双方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paired Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (or rectangular mound), measuring about 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width. Each mound is believed to contain similar-sized horizontal stone chambers. According to legend, this tomb is shared by Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Echizen Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} : A rectangular mound measuring approximately 75 meters east-to-west, 55 meters north-to-south, and 8 meters in height. The western side of the mound is significantly damaged. Smaller associated Kofuns include: :* Mondo Kamizuka Kofun (round) :* {{ill|Kamado Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} (round) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (undetermined) :* Subordinate mounds associated with larger burials
== Notes == The term "Okutsuki Kofun" is used to describe a Kofun in Shinagadani, which has been alternatively identified as another name for the Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun (Emperor Bidatsu’s tomb)<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website])</small> or Prince Shōtoku’s mausoleum in certain sources.

[[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11588120}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Taishi|WD=Q1347173}}|part_of={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|WD=Q48743902}}; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|WD=Q11371443}}; {{ill|Goryōyama Kofun|WD=Q48753099}}; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Cluster|WD=Q48753076}}; {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11513590}}; {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|WD=Q63386720}}; {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|WD=Q48751982}}; {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|WD=Q11470748}}; {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|WD=Q48752311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11588120]] Wikidata name: Shinagadani Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}. These Kofuns date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)]] : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)

=== Major Kofuns === The following five Kofuns have been designated as [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred Individual !! Mound Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] || Keyhole-shaped || 93 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] || Square || 63 m × 60 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] || Square || 63 m × 56 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] || Round || 30 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] || Round || 55 m || Equivalent to an imperial tomb |}
=== Other Notable Kofuns === ; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Taishi, Osaka)]] : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of Empress Suiko’s tomb (Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun), this mound is a {{ill|paired Kofun|ja|双方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paired Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (or rectangular mound), measuring about 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width. Each mound is believed to contain similar-sized horizontal stone chambers. According to legend, this tomb is shared by Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Echizen Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} : A rectangular mound measuring approximately 75 meters east-to-west, 55 meters north-to-south, and 8 meters in height. The western side of the mound is significantly damaged. Smaller associated Kofuns include: :* Mondo Kamizuka Kofun (round) :* {{ill|Kamado Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} (round) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (undetermined) :* Subordinate mounds associated with larger burials
== Notes == The term "Okutsuki Kofun" is used to describe a Kofun in Shinagadani, which has been alternatively identified as another name for the Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun (Emperor Bidatsu’s tomb)<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website])</small> or Prince Shōtoku’s mausoleum in certain sources.

[[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11588120}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Taishi|WD=Q1347173}}|part_of={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|WD=Q48743902}}; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|WD=Q11371443}}; {{ill|Goryōyama Kofun|WD=Q48753099}}; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Cluster|WD=Q48753076}}; {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11513590}}; {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|WD=Q63386720}}; {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|WD=Q48751982}}; {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|WD=Q11470748}}; {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|WD=Q48752311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588120]] Wikidata name: Shinagadani Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}. These Kofuns date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)]] : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
== References ==
* ''Exploring Kawachi Asuka'' (edited by Masashi Ishibe, Shōraisha, ISBN 4-87984-101-3)

[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns centered on imperial tombs, located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group consists of four imperial tombs designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]—{{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Bidatsu), {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei), {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko), and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kōtoku)—as well as approximately 30 other Kofuns, including the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}, said to be the tomb of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. These Kofuns date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, spanning the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Notably, the mound attributed to Emperor Bidatsu is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') within this group.
The four imperial tombs and Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum are collectively referred to as the "'''Plum Blossom Petal Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). The area, situated on the broad valley slopes of the {{ill|Nijō Mountains|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Mountains|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is known as Shinagadani. Due to the concentration of royal tombs, it is also called the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns === The following five Kofuns have been designated as [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred Individual !! Mound Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] || Keyhole-shaped || 93 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] || Square || 63 m × 60 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] || Square || 63 m × 56 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] || Round || 30 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] || Round || 55 m || Equivalent to an imperial tomb |}

Each mound is believed to contain similar-sized horizontal stone chambers. According to legend, this tomb is shared by Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|qq=|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Echizen Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} : A rectangular mound measuring approximately 75 meters east-to-west, 55 meters north-to-south, and 8 meters in height. The western side of the mound is significantly damaged. Smaller associated Kofuns include: :* Mondo Kamizuka Kofun (round) :* {{ill|Kamado Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} (round) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (undetermined) :* Subordinate mounds associated with larger burials
== Notes == The term "Okutsuki Kofun" is used to describe a Kofun in Shinagadani, which has been alternatively identified as another name for the Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun (Emperor Bidatsu’s tomb)<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website])</small> or Prince Shōtoku’s mausoleum in certain sources.

[[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taishi, Ōsaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinagadani Kofun Cluster]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11588120}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Taishi|WD=Q1347173}}|part_of={{ill|Eifukuji Kita Kofun|WD=Q48743902}}; {{ill|Futagozuka Kofun|WD=Q11371443}}; {{ill|Goryōyama Kofun|WD=Q48753099}}; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Cluster|WD=Q48753076}}; {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11513590}}; {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|WD=Q63386720}}; {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|WD=Q48751982}}; {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|WD=Q11470748}}; {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|WD=Q48752311}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11588120]] Wikidata name: Shinagadani Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a group of [[Kofun|Kofuns]] centered around [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. == Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group comprises approximately 30 Kofuns, including four mausoleums designated as [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] ({{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|lt_ja=用明天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|孝徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}), along with the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|lt_ja=聖徳太子墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}. These Kofuns date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)]] : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>

[[ja:磯長谷古墳群]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== '''Shinagadani Kofun Group''' (磯長谷古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns centered on imperial tombs, located in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The Shinagadani Kofun Group consists of four imperial tombs designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]—{{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Bidatsu), {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Yōmei), {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Empress Suiko), and {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Emperor Kōtoku)—as well as approximately 30 other Kofuns, including the {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}, said to be the tomb of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]. These Kofuns date from the mid-6th century to the early 7th century, spanning the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] and the beginning of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. Notably, the mound attributed to Emperor Bidatsu is the only keyhole-shaped Kofun (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') within this group.
The four imperial tombs and Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum are collectively referred to as the "'''Plum Blossom Petal Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵). The area, situated on the broad valley slopes of the {{ill|Nijō Mountains|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Mountains|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is known as Shinagadani. Due to the concentration of royal tombs, it is also called the "'''Valley of Royal Mausoleums'''" (王陵の谷).
=== Major Kofuns === The following five Kofuns have been designated as [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] by the Imperial Household Agency:
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred Individual !! Mound Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|1=Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] || Keyhole-shaped || 93 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|1=Kasugayama Kofun|1=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|2=ja|3=春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] || Square || 63 m × 60 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] || Square || 63 m × 56 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]] || Round || 30 m || Imperial tomb |- | {{ill|Eifukuji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifukuji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] || Round || 55 m || Equivalent to an imperial tomb |}
=== Other Notable Kofuns === ; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Taishi, Osaka)]] : Located approximately 200 meters southeast of Empress Suiko’s tomb (Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun), this mound is a {{ill|paired Kofun|ja|双方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paired Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (or rectangular mound), measuring about 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width. Each mound is believed to contain similar-sized horizontal stone chambers. According to legend, this tomb is shared by Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Echizen Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} : A rectangular mound measuring approximately 75 meters east-to-west, 55 meters north-to-south, and 8 meters in height. The western side of the mound is significantly damaged. Smaller associated Kofuns include: :* Mondo Kamizuka Kofun (round) :* {{ill|Kamado Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群|1=Hamuro Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48753076}} (round) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamuro Ishizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (undetermined) :* Subordinate mounds associated with larger burials
== Notes == The term "Okutsuki Kofun" is used to describe a Kofun in Shinagadani, which has been alternatively identified as another name for the Prince Taishi Nishiyama Kofun (Emperor Bidatsu’s tomb)<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website])</small> or Prince Shōtoku’s mausoleum in certain sources.

These Kofuns date to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], between the mid-6th and early 7th centuries. Notably, the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} is the only [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] within this group.
The five main tumuli (four Imperial mausolea and Prince Shōtoku's tomb) are collectively referred to as the "'''Umebachi Mausoleums'''" (梅鉢御陵), likened to the petals of a plum blossom. The area, located on the broad valley slopes of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, is also known as the "'''Valley of Royal Tombs'''." (王陵の谷).
=== Main Tumuli === The following five tumuli are designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as Imperial mausolea: {| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Interred !! Shape !! Length !! Notes |- | {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]]|| Keyhole-shaped || 93m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|春日向山古墳|4=de|5=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|6=fr|7=Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Mukaiyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]|| Square || 63m × 60m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|ja|山田高塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]|| Square || 63m × 56m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|ja|山田上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Uenoyama Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]]|| Round || 30m || Imperial mausoleum |- | {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}}|| [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]|| Round || 55m || Equivalent to an Imperial mausoleum |} :
=== Other Kofuns ===
Significant Kofuns in the area include:
; [[:en:Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)|Futagozuka Kofun (Osaka)]] : Located about 200 meters southeast of the Kasuga Mukoyama Kofun, this double Kofun (also referred to as a {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) measures approximately 60 meters in length and 25 meters in width, with a mound height of about 6–4.8 meters. It is believed to contain two nearly identical horizontal stone chambers. There is a tradition that this is the joint tomb of Empress Suiko and {{ill|Prince Takeda|ja|竹田皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takeda|1=Prince Takeda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599359}}.
; {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamurozuka Kofun|ja|葉室塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamurozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} : A rectangular mound measuring 75 meters east–west and 55 meters north–south, with a mound height of approximately 8 meters. The western portion of the mound is significantly damaged. Surrounding the Hamurozuka Kofun are smaller Kofuns, including: :* Mondō Kamizuka Kofun (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Kamadozuka Kofun|ja|釜戸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamadozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Round mound) :* {{ill|Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamuro Kofun Group|lt=Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja|葉室石塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamusho Ishizuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葉室古墳群}} (Unknown type) :* Associated tumuli (smaller mounds connected to larger tombs)
== Notes ==
The name "Okuno Kofun" refers to a Kofun in the Shinagadani area. While some sources identify it as an alternative name for the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}, others associate it with the mausoleum of Prince Shōtoku.<small>Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town Website]</small>
== Notes == The Isonokami Valley is home to a tumulus known as "Okushiro Kofun," which is sometimes identified with the {{ill|Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|ja|太子西山古墳|敏達天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishi Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}} or with Prince Shōtoku's mausoleum, depending on the source.<small> (Reference: [http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/sightseeing/rekishi/rekishi04.html Taishi Town website])</small>
